rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Mtn To Compel Pltff'S Supplemental Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Demands For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions (Ccp 2023.030, 2030.290, 2030.300, 2031.300 & 2033.90)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 18, 2013, Line 29, DEFENDANTS COUNTY OF SAN MATEO, CELIA MORENO, M.D.'S Second Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Supplemental Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Demands For Production Of Documents, And For Sanctions (Ccp 2023.030, 2030.290, 2030.300, 2031.300 & 2033.90) Defendants' motion is granted in part and denied part. The motion is granted as to Supplemental Response Interrogatories Nos. 202.1, 204.3, 204.6, 208.1, 210.2, 210.3, 210.5, 210.6, 212.4, 212.5 and 212.7. Plaintiff is to provide complete responses as to these interrogatories. Plaintiff is to produce any documents not already produced that are identified in the above responses. Responses are due no later than 30 days from the date of this order. Sanctions are denied. The motion is denied in all other respects. The Court does not provide a court reporter for this discovery motion. If the parties wish the matter to be reported, they must meet and confer and arrange for a reporter to be present. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $778.75 U.S. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 18, 2013, Line 1: Petitioner PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion for Order Terminating Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Peter VanZandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion by Petitioner, People of the State of California for terminating sanctions and to strike the claim of Joshua M. Sheedar is GRANTED. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm one day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MANZAR DOKHT AZARI VS. OFFICE OF ADMINISTRATIVE HEARINGS, A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition And Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus And Ntc Of Hearing And Lodging Of Administrative Record
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 18, 2013, Line 7, PETITIONER MANZAR AZARI'S Notice Of Petition And Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus And Notice Of Hearing And Lodging Of Administrative Record Denied. The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. Ms. Azari appears to concede that she violated numerous provisions of the statutes and regulations imposing mandatory requirements on real estate broker. Even if Ms. Azari did not so concede, per the independent judgment standard of review, the Commissioner's findings are amply supported by the weight of the evidence, especially since the governing law does not require that the violations be intentionally committed to support disciplinary action. The only real issue on this writ is whether the Commissioner's decision to revoke Ms. Azari's license constitutes an abuse of discretion. Both sides recognize that relief is available to Ms. Azari only where all "reasonable minds" would agree that a lesser penalty rather than revocation was appropriate given the nature of the violations and surrounding circumstances. Here, while revocation of Ms. Azari's license is arguably harsh, it cannot be said that all reasonable minds would agree that some lesser penalty was more appropriate. The violations go to the heart of the fiduciary nature of being a real estate broker and evidence, at the very least, a troubling lack of understanding of basic fiduciary principles. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. To repeat, the hearing will commence at 9:00 a.m. =(302/HEK)

The motion asks the Court to "enforce the verification requirement of Claimant's interest in the property." Motion at 7. However, Plaintiff's claim is verified. (Exhibit D.) The motion seeks to require plaintiff to state the nature of his interest in the property. However, he has done so: "My wife gave me the money to hold." (Exhibit D, page 2.) =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GUERNEVILLE BUSINESS CORPORATION, INC. A VS. ALBERT L BOASBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 18, 2013, Line 18, CROSS DEFENDANT GUERNEVILLE BUSINESS CORPORATION, INC.'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Hearing required. The Court is inclined to rule as follows: As to the first cause of action, sustain with leave to amend to allege facts supporting malice and lack of probable cause, and that the underlying litigation has concluded. Bertero v. National General Corp. (1974) 872 Cal. 3d 43, 50. As to the second cause of action, sustain the demurrer without leave to amend because this claim is barred by the litigation privilege and cross-complainant did not contest this basis for demurrer. Kimmel v. Goland (1990) 51 Cal. 3d 202, 209. As to the third cause of action, the Court requires the parties to address the status of Scott Mandell's and Ray Shahani's bankruptcies. By Court order filed April 22, 2013, the action was stayed as to Scott Mandell on account of bankruptcy. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CASTO TRAVEL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. BOONE, JOHN WINSTON, AKA JOHN MCELROY et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 18, 2013, Line 23, DEFENDANT JOHN BOONE'S Motion To Stay Action Hearing required. Judge Miller discloses and will disclose at the hearing that she was in the same Inn of Court as plaintiff's counsel Marc Lewis and Paul Llewellyn. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. The Court is inclined to grant the motion staying this case in light of the pending criminal investigation. See Pacers Inc. v. Superior Court (1984) 162 Cal. App. 3d 686. Plaintiff is requested to address whether and where defendant is in custody so that if he is unrepresented and not present at the hearing, arrangements can be made for a possible future telephonic hearing on this motion. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN TERENZIO et al VS. CURRENT TV, LLC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defts To Compel Further Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 18, 2013, Line 7: Defendants CURRENT TV, LLC'S, CURRENT MEDIA, LLC'S, and AL GORE'S Motion to Compel Further Production of Documents Pro Tem Judge Peter VanZandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants Current TV, LLC, CurrentMedia, LLC and Al Gore's Motion to Compel Further Production of Documents is DENIED. Evidence Code §771 is not so broad as to excuse counsel from asking specific factual questions at deposition and then inquire about whether any document had informed the deponent Terenzio's answers. Here, Defendants are in essence, seeking a blanket production of all documents reviewed by Terenzio in advance of his deposition which are protected by the attorney work product doctrine. The Court is persuaded that the email correspondence is protected under the "common interest" and "joint representation doctrines." Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm one day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
